info.name: D. Pricing Management 🏷️
info.description:
# Pricing Management 🏷️

## Description

Stay competitive on Amazon with automated pricing workflows. This collection walks you through retrieving competitive offers, analyzing pricing data, and updating your prices — all using the Product Pricing and Listings APIs.

## Overview

Pricing is one of the key levers sellers have to win the Featured Offer and drive sales. The [Product Pricing API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/product-pricing-api-v2022-05-01-reference) provides access to competitive pricing data, while the [Listings Items API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/listings-items-api-v2021-08-01-reference) lets you push price updates programmatically.

This collection combines both APIs into practical workflows — from retrieving competitive summaries to repricing your offers.

## SP-APIs Involved

| API | Version | Documentation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Product Pricing API | v2022-05-01 | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/product-pricing-api-v2022-05-01-reference) |
| Listings Items API | v2021-08-01 | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/listings-items-api-v2021-08-01-reference) |
| Product Type Definitions API | v2020-09-01 | [API Reference](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/product-type-definitions-api-v2020-09-01-reference) |

## Use Cases

| Folder | Description |
| --- | --- |
| \[Pricing API\] Recipes | Common pricing tasks and quick-reference recipes |
| \[Pricing Flow\] Offer Analysis & Repricing | Retrieve competitive pricing data and update your offer prices |

## Getting Started

### Prerequisites

- A registered SP-API application ([How to register](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/registering-your-application))
    
- Valid SP-API credentials (LWA client ID and secret)
    
- A seller account authorized to your application
    

### Setup Steps

1. **Fork the collection** — Click "Fork" on the collection to create your own copy.
    
2. **Fork the environment** — Fork the associated [environment template](https://www.postman.com/amazon-selling-partner-api/sp-api/environment/) as well.
    
3. **Set environment variables** — Fill in your `client_id`, `client_secret`, `refresh_token`, `marketplace`, `seller_id`, and `base_url` in the environment.
    
4. **Assign the environment** — Select the forked environment from the environment dropdown in Postman.
    
5. **Start exploring** — The collection includes a pre-request script that automatically handles authentication.
    

## Additional Resources

- [SP-API Documentation](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/)
    
- [Product Pricing API Use Case Guide](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/product-pricing-api-v2022-05-01-use-case-guide)
    
- [Marketplace IDs](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/marketplace-ids)

Manage Amazon Automate Pricing Rules for an offer
Step 1: Retrieve seller Automated Pricing Rules

query.description: (Required) A comma-delimited list of Amazon marketplace identifiers for the request.

A locale for localization of issues. When not provided, the default language code of the first marketplace is used. Examples: "en_US", "fr_CA", "fr_FR". Localized messages default to "en_US" when a localization is not available in the specified locale.

variable.description: (Required) A selling partner identifier, such as a merchant account or vendor code.
(Required) A selling partner provided identifier for an Amazon listing.

request.description:
Partially update (patch) a listings item for a selling partner. Only top-level listings item attributes can be patched. Patching nested attributes is not supported.

**Note:** The parameters associated with this operation may contain special characters that must be encoded to successfully call the API. To avoid errors with SKUs when encoding URLs, refer to [URL Encoding](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/url-encoding).

**Usage Plan:**

| Rate (requests per second) | Burst |
| ---- | ---- |
| 5 | 10 |

The `x-amzn-RateLimit-Limit` response header returns the usage plan rate limits that were applied to the requested operation, when available. The table above indicates the default rate and burst values for this operation. Selling partners whose business demands require higher throughput may see higher rate and burst values than those shown here. For more information, see [Usage Plans and Rate Limits in the Selling Partner API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/usage-plans-and-rate-limits-in-the-sp-api).

item.description:
## Overview

With Definitions and Listings APIs you now can enroll sellers SKU into Amazon Automate Pricing rules tool. This gives the ability to leverage on Amazon repricing system.

### Basic Workflow

1. Call getDefinitionsProductType API to retrieve the sellers rules and fetch automated_pricing_merchandising_rule_plan from the product schema.
    
2. Extract the enum rules e.g. `328182282407-COMPETITIVE_BUYBOX` = `Competitive Price Rule by Amazon`
    
3. Assign the rule by using patchListingsItem, setting also Min and Max prices guardrails.
    

Documentation details: [Manage automated pricing rules with SP-API](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/manage-automated-pricing-rules)

item.name: Step 3: Check Featured Offer Expected Price from highest GVWP location

request.description:
Returns the set of responses that correspond to the batched list of up to 40 requests defined in the request body. The response for each successful (HTTP status code 200) request in the set includes the computed listing price at or below which a seller can expect to become the featured offer (before applicable promotions). This is called the featured offer expected price (FOEP). Featured offer is not guaranteed because competing offers might change. Other offers might be featured based on factors such as fulfillment capabilities to a specific customer. The response to an unsuccessful request includes the available error text.

**Usage Plan:**

| Rate (requests per second) | Burst |
| ---- | ---- |
| 0.033 | 1 |

The `x-amzn-RateLimit-Limit` response header returns the usage plan rate limits that are applied to the requested operation, when available. The preceding table contains the default rate and burst values for this operation. Selling partners whose business demands require higher throughput may receive higher rate and burst values than those shown here. For more information, refer to [Usage Plans and Rate Limits](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api/docs/usage-plans-and-rate-limits-in-the-sp-api) in the Selling Partner API.

item.description:
## Overview

The getCompetitiveSummary operation returns multiple postal codes with their associated Glance View Weight Percentage values which indicates the percentage of total glance views for an ASIN in that location, helping you identify where customer interest is highest.

Using this concept this use case will focus on how you can get featured offer expected price, based on a location where there are more glance view weight percentages.

### Basic Workflow

1. Call getCompetitiveSummary with an ASIN to analyze the Glance View Weight Percentage (GVWP) by location
    
2. Extract the postal code from the segment with the highest GVWP
    
3. Use that postal code to call getFeaturedOfferExpectedPriceBatch to get location-specific pricing.
    
4. Adjust prices using patchListingsItem based on the response from getFeaturedOfferExpectedPriceBatch

Multiple tasks can be achieved using the Pricing APIs. This collection is a cheatsheet-like resource that guides you into how the API is used to execute changes such as: - Update price using sample location - Get Amazon Featured Offer Expected Price for a batch of SKUs **Authorization** The APIs in this collection require the _**x-amz-access-token**_ header in order for SP-API to authorize requests. The collection comes with a pre-script which calls the OAuth API to retrieve the access token and then stores it in the _{{access_token}}_ variable. The `Environments` tab in this workspace comes with environments for each API region and can be used with this collection. Make sure to select or fork the environment and fill in the values. **Variables** Variables configured for this collection are: - Authorization - client_id - client_secret - refresh_token - access_token - Pricing recipes - sellerId - marketplace

exec: //retrieve and store access tokens using the authentication endpoint
//the access_token variable is then referenced in the x-amz-access-token header.

item.description: ## 👋 Introduction Welcome to Amazon's Collection for Pricing with Selling Partner APIs. This collection provides an easy setup to retrieve offers using the **getCompetitiveSummary** of the Product Pricing API v2022-05-01 and send price updates using the Listings API **patchListingsItem** operation. ### 💡 Flows x Collection A flow comes with this collection: [[Pricing] Dashboard Prototype + Repricing](https://amazon-selling-partner-api.postman.co/workspace/SP-API~31be1efb-d4eb-4706-8b4c-7588e426cdb6/flow/672dfe7075516a22fad02fa1) With the Pricing Flow you will be able to test the getCompetitiveSummary API, with the response create a simple dashboard, and finally we submit a price update for a SKU using the competitive price threshold. Collections contain all the requests and documentation associated with that API. ### How to use the Pricing flow: 1. Fork the [Flow](https://amazon-selling-partner-api.postman.co/workspace/SP-API~31be1efb-d4eb-4706-8b4c-7588e426cdb6/flow/672dfe7075516a22fad02fa1). 2. Make sure you set the environment for all REQUEST blocks (getCompetitiveSummary and patchListingsItem). 3\. In the getCompetitiveSummary block you should include the ASIN information and marketplace id (e.g. ATVPDKIKX0DER) 4\. Include the marketplace id (e.g. ATVPDKIKX0DER) and sellerId in Listings API as well: 5\. Hit the **Run button** to test the flow. ### **Reference** collections These collections contain all the requests and documentation associated with the Pricing and Listings APIs. Please share this with customers to provide a comprehensive overview of our APIs and how to use them. ## **🛟 Help and support** If you are blocked or have any questions when using this workspace, please check out the following channels: - [SP-API Documentation.](https://developer-docs.amazon.com/sp-api) - [SP-API Developer Community](https://github.com/amzn/selling-partner-api-samples/discussions).
